title: market-categorizer output
description: Output of the f04 market-categorizer agent (gpt-5.6-luna). data.category is written verbatim as the value JSONB of the market.category claim (design.md 3.2). No field in this schema may contain a numeric quantity: this agent runs before any web search and therefore holds no source_url for any number (design.md 9).

code.description: Finite set; the agent may not invent codes. 'abstained' is a COMPLETED job, not a failure (design.md 9: abstention is explicitly permitted).

ok.description: true for both 'success' and 'abstained'; false for 'insufficient_input' and 'processing_error'. Consumer: the n8n IF node that routes to the gap-claim writer vs the error branch (design.md 4).

message.description: Human-readable. Required non-empty for 'abstained', 'insufficient_input' and 'processing_error'; empty string for 'success'.

category.description: Written verbatim as claims.value for topic 'market.category', axis 'market' (design.md 3.2). All six keys present in every response, including abstentions.

canonical.description: Normalised category label, reused character-for-character from known_categories when a synonym exists, otherwise newly minted (design.md 9). Consumers: query templates Q2/Q3/Q5 (design.md 4), companies.category normalisation, memo category chip (design.md 11 / feature 06), dashboard Market tab chip (feature 09). null only when the agent abstained.

raw.description: The company's own category words, untidied. Kept alongside canonical because a fixed taxonomy alone forces novel startups into legacy buckets (design.md 9). Consumers: memo (feature 06), dashboard (feature 09).

adjacent.description: Neighbouring markets whose incumbents could serve the same buyer. Consumer: the adjacent-entrant discovery searches in f04-competition-intel (design.md 8). Empty array is valid; padding is a defect.

icp.description: One sentence naming who pays and what qualifies them as a buyer. MUST NOT contain a quantity. Consumers: memo Company snapshot / Market sizing narrative (feature 06).

buyer_unit.description: The countable noun a statistics office, trade association or registry would publish a count of. Consumer: query templates Q1 ('how many {buyer_unit} in {geography}') and Q2 ('{category} pricing per {buyer_unit} annual cost'), design.md 4. null when no countable noun is defensible; the query builder then SKIPS Q1/Q2 rather than substituting a generic noun, and the TAM band ends UNKNOWN (design.md 6.0/6.1).

buyer_concentration.description: NON-AUTHORITATIVE QUERY HINT ONLY. The AUTHORITATIVE value is derived deterministically in the validator from the sizer's evidence-backed buyer_count using the < 10k / 10k-500k / > 500k tiers (design.md 6.2). On disagreement the derived value wins and missing_flags.concentration_revised = true. Never consumed as a finding; never used to set share_assumption directly. Emitted here only to shape pre-search query phrasing. 'unknown' is the correct value whenever buyer_unit is null or two bands are plausible.

gaps.description: MANDATORY explicit gap declaration (design.md 9 — the deliberate inversion of company-research-agent's 'never mention no information found'). Every null or 'unknown' field above MUST have a matching entry. Consumers: the *.gap claim writer (design.md 3.2, verification_status='missing') and scores.missing_flags (design.md 3.7).

reason_code.description: Closed vocabulary so the validator can map gaps to missing_flags keys deterministically. The agent may not invent codes.

note.description: Optional free text explaining the gap. Rendered in the memo's honest-omission section (feature 06).

category.raw: automated month-end close for community banks
category.icp: US community banks under $2B in assets with an in-house controller

status.message: No product description; one-liner is a slogan with no product content.

gaps.note: Slogan only; no deck claims present (radar_activated entry).
No buyer unit established, so no band can be hinted.

status.message: company_name, one_liner and deck_claims all empty.
